title: Coding Prompts, Scoped Edits, and Regression Control · Prompt Defense
summary: Implement a repository change with tests and minimal unrelated churn. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a coding-agent implementation brief. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Name desired behavior, relevant area, preserved contracts, and authorized refactor scope.
Ground implementation in repository commands, architecture, dependency policy, and local instructions.
Specify risk-proportionate tests and observable regression checks.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

quiz case — Use a changed quiz case for prompt coding edits that tests this competency: defend the module contract. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Change Scope evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Change Scope assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write the quiz evidence note for Implementation Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Use a changed quiz case for prompt coding edits that tests this competency: Require command output, behavior checks, and a concise unresolved-risk handoff.. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
steps.why: Retrieving Implementation Evidence through the concrete a coding-agent implementation brief case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Require command output, behavior checks, and a concise unresolved-risk handoff.
